name: Red Sea / Suez Canal
summary: Day 103 / 2026-06-10 (Wednesday): **HOUTHI 'COMPLETE BAN ON ENEMY NAVIGATION' (RHETORIC) AFTER THE JUNE 8-9 STRIKES; A JUNE 10 SMALL-BOAT INCIDENT SW OF BALHAF (UNATTRIBUTED, LIKELY PIRACY) — STILL NO CONFIRMED HOUTHI ATTACK ON NEUTRAL SHIPPING.** Three-day window (June 7 cutoff -> early June 10; June 8/9 runs empty). The Houthis declared a 'complete ban on enemy navigation' and signalled renewed Red Sea attacks following the June 8-9 US/Israeli strikes on Iran (gCaptain) — RHETORIC. The only operational event: UKMTO reported June 10 a cargo vessel southwest of Balhaf, Yemen approached by a small craft with six armed individuals; the embarked security team exchanged fire and the attackers broke off (no casualties, no damage, attribution UNDER INVESTIGATION — The National) — a small-boat profile reading as likely PIRACY, NOT a Houthi missile/drone strike; do NOT attribute to Houthis. UKMTO continues to describe the region as highly volatile with sustained GNSS/AIS/VHF interference. No confirmed Houthi attack on neutral shipping. **TRIGGER DECISIONS (June 10)**: the 'Houthis attack commercial shipping in the Red Sea' trigger STAYS watching (watch elevated on the 'ban' rhetoric; the Balhaf incident is unattributed/likely piracy, not a qualifying confirmed Houthi strike). No edge weight change. PRIOR — Day 99 (Saturday June 6; covers 4 June afternoon -> 5 June US session -> 5-6 June). **CARRY-FORWARD — CORRIDOR STAYS ACTION-QUIET; REZAEI NAMED THE RED SEA IN HIS WIDER-WAR THEATER MAP (RHETORIC/THREAT), NO DATED KINETIC EVENT.** No verified new kinetic strike on commercial shipping in the corridor this window — the most-threatened second chokepoint stays quiet. STATED-RISK OVERLAY (no action): Iran adviser Rezaei named the Red Sea (alongside the Indian Ocean and Mediterranean) in a wider-war theater map — RHETORIC/THREAT, which widens the STATED risk surface but is not an act. The double-chokepoint (Red Sea + Hormuz) wildcard stays at watching; no weight change. PRIOR — Day 96 (Wednesday June 3; covers 2 June afternoon -> 3 June morning). **BAB EL-MANDEB STAYED ACTION-QUIET ON THE CYCLE'S FIRST CASUALTY DAY — STILL NO CONFIRMED NEW ATTACK DESPITE THE 'ACTIVATION' RHETORIC (CONFIRMED NON-EVENT).** The 3 June barrage and renewed IRGC/Houthi Bab-el-Mandeb threats produced no verified kinetic strike on commercial shipping in the corridor. A MARAD advisory (2026-006) and standing UKMTO reporting note elevated risk and 'significant military activity'; one gCaptain report of a merchant vessel repelling an armed approach off Yemen exists, but no confirmed Houthi strike on neutral shipping has been verified. The most-threatened second chokepoint stays quiet — the non-event is the intelligence. The double-chokepoint (Red Sea + Hormuz) wildcard stays at watching with elevated rhetorical pressure but no action. No weight change. PRIOR — Day 95 (Monday June 2; covers 30 May afternoon -> 2 June morning). **BAB EL-MANDEB NOW EXPLICITLY IN THE THREAT SET — BUT STILL NO CONFIRMED NEW ATTACK (CONFIRMED NON-EVENT). Tied to Iran's 1 June suspension of the US talks, Tasnim vowed the 'resistance front' would 'activate the Bab al-Mandab,' and the IRGC Quds Force commander threatened to create a 'traffic situation' in BOTH Bab el-Mandeb and Hormuz (CONFIRMED the threat was made; closure unimplemented). The corridor stayed action-quiet: no confirmed new merchant-vessel strike this window — coverage is forecast/threat framing, and circulating 'confirmed attack' items trace to earlier dates (July Magic Seas later/separate; the 'Houthis end attacks' headline is the May 2025 Oman pause — stale-source trap avoided). The companion Houthi Haifa-shipping kinetic trigger (tracked on the houthis node) reached its deadline 2 June and EXPIRED UNTRIPPED — a confirmed non-event. The double-chokepoint (Red Sea + Hormuz) wildcard stays at watching, now with elevated rhetorical pressure but no action. No weight change.** Day 94 (Wednesday May 27; ~48h coverage, no brief May 26). **HOUTHI HAIFA 'MARITIME BLOCKADE' FALSIFIED — THE LLOYD'S JWC EASTERN-MED/HAIFA 7-DAY TRIGGER EXPIRED UNTRIPPED MAY 26 (CONFIRMED NON-EVENT).** No confirmed JWC listing (Listed Areas still at JWLA-033, March 3, 2026) and no confirmed kinetic attack on a Haifa-bound vessel reached the May 26 deadline; the Lloyd's-JWC Eastern-Med/Haifa trigger (tracked on the houthis node) moved `watching` → resolved (expired untripped). Analysts (Maritime Executive) note the Houthis lack the naval reach to enforce a Mediterranean blockade directly and can only pressure shipowners who choose to call at Haifa — the cleanest external falsification of the May 19 rhetoric. Negative-space verification holds: no kinetic Red Sea incident beyond the May 11 unclaimed Hodeidah event, and no confirmed Haifa attack. The companion Houthi-kinetic-on-Haifa-bound-shipping trigger stays `watching` ~Day 8 of 14 (by 2026-06-02). The ~48h kinetic escalation is on the IRAN axis (US strikes on IRGC mine-laying boats in Hormuz + a Bandar Abbas SAM site) and the LEBANON front — NEITHER is a Red-Sea action; the corridor stays quiet on this thread. A CONFIRMED non-event generates no edge weight changes. PRIOR (May 25): **HOUTHI HAIFA 'MARITIME BLOCKADE' DAY 7 OF 14 — RHETORIC-ONLY TAG HOLDS; NO CONFIRMED HOUTHI KINETIC ATTACK ON A HAIFA-BOUND VESSEL THROUGH MAY 25 (CONFIRMED NON-EVENT). LLOYD'S JWC LISTED AREAS REMAIN AT JWLA-033 (MARCH 3, 2026) WITH NO EASTERN MED / HAIFA ADDITION — DAY 7 OF 7, ON TRACK TO EXPIRE UNTRIPPED TOMORROW (MAY 26).** The insurance market's refusal to list the Eastern Med remains the cleanest external falsification of the Haifa-blockade rhetoric; the Houthis 'have not resumed attacks since the start of the current confrontation' despite retaining capability. Negative-space verification holds: no kinetic Red Sea incident beyond the May 11 unclaimed Hodeidah event. The Lloyd's-JWC-Eastern-Med trigger is tracked on the houthis node (Day 7 of 7, by 2026-05-26) and is kept at `watching` — it expires TOMORROW, not today, so it is NOT marked tripped; on track to close UNTRIPPED. The day's regional escalation is on the LEBANON front (Hezbollah 1,000+ drones / 700+ rockets over 8 days, US weighing a greenlight for an expanded Israeli operation) — a separate axis, NOT a Red-Sea action; the Red Sea / Suez corridor stays quiet. PRIOR (May 23): **HOUTHI HAIFA 'MARITIME BLOCKADE' DAY 5 OF 14 — RHETORIC-ONLY TAG HOLDS; NO CONFIRMED KINETIC ATTACK ON HAIFA-BOUND VESSEL MAY 20-23 IN MAJOR MARITIME TRACKING OUTLETS. LLOYD'S JWC DAY 5 OF 7 WITH NO EASTERN MED LISTING CHANGE — INSURANCE MARKET NOT BUYING THE THREAT.** The Loadstar May 22-23 introduces a new tangent — Houthis may target 'Egypt's Red Sea ports' — but remains rhetoric only without operational confirmation. Lloyd's JWLA-033 (March 3, 2026) remains current published listing; no Eastern Med addition through May 23. AWRP ~1% holding from May 19 levels (8x pre-war). **The Houthi proxy network continues to demonstrate graduated restraint inside the Gulf-mediated deal window** — Iran-aligned signal that rhetoric coexists with deal architecture. Container freight: no fresh FBX print (Freightos terminal pull needed Monday). France Charles de Gaulle CSG continues Red Sea presence. **Negative-space verification holds**: no kinetic Red Sea incident May 20-23 beyond the May 11 unclaimed Hodeidah event. PRIOR (May 22): **HOUTHI HAIFA 'MARITIME BLOCKADE' DAY 4 OF 14 — RHETORIC-ONLY TAG HOLDS; NO CONFIRMED KINETIC ATTACK ON HAIFA-BOUND VESSEL; LLOYD'S JWC HAS NOT ADDED EASTERN MED.** Three days after the May 19 announcement (Houthi spokesman Saree), no confirmed kinetic attack on a Haifa-bound vessel has surfaced in major maritime tracking outlets (Maritime Executive, Ship & Bunker, Seatrade Maritime, Supply Chain Brain). **Lloyd's Joint War Committee Listed Areas update JWLA-033 (March 3, 2026)** added Bahrain/Djibouti/Kuwait/Oman/Qatar to Persian Gulf coverage — but NO subsequent update has added Eastern Mediterranean or Haifa. **Lloyd's JWC Eastern Med trigger remains `watching` Day 4 of 7**; confirmed kinetic attack trigger remains `watching` Day 4 of 14. **AWRP ~1% holding from May 19 levels** (Argus, Lloyd's List) — insurers have NOT bought the deal-anticipation tape yet; physical premium intact. **The Houthi proxy network is demonstrating graduated restraint inside the Gulf-mediated deal window** — Iran-aligned signal that rhetoric can coexist with deal architecture. Container freight: no fresh FBX print in this window. France Charles de Gaulle CSG continues Red Sea presence per prior. **The negative-space verification holds**: no kinetic Red Sea incident May 19-22 beyond the May 11 unclaimed Hodeidah event. PRIOR (May 19): **A4 NEGATIVE-SPACE WINDOW DAY 7 OF 7 CLOSES UNTRIPPED** — no second incident confirmed since May 11 Hodeidah; UKMTO recent-incidents page shows no new advisory through Monday morning IST; MARAD 2026-006 stands without supplement. **May 11 Hodeidah unclaimed incident reverts to 'single unclaimed event,' not start of new pattern.** Trigger on strait-of-hormuz node ('Second Red Sea shipping incident within 7 days of May 11 Hodeidah-area UKMTO advisory') moves watching → untripped_expired. **Houthi non-action during publicly-hardening US kinetic-preparation phase is the cleanest behavioural divergence from March-April pattern**: NYT preparation report May 16, Trump 'clock is ticking' May 17, Trump-Netanyahu call May 17, Saudi drone interception May 17, UAE Barakah drone strike May 17. Pair with TASS/Xinhua non-amplification + Mojtaba Day 11 silence + Hezbollah calibrated attrition = **coordinated quietism across Iran's proxy and patron networks against US kinetic noise**. Investor read: Iran signalling escalation control, not escalation. Container freight FBX01 Asia-US WC ~+2% w/w; Asia-US East Coast +10% same week; Asia-N Europe -3%; Asia-Med +7%. France Charles de Gaulle CSG continues Red Sea presence per prior. **PRIOR (May 17): Day 85 (Sunday). NEGATIVE-SPACE HOLDS DAY 6 OF 7 since May 11 Hodeidah unclaimed incident — UKMTO 'Recent Incidents' page shows NO second incident through May 17 morning IST.** If no incident by ~May 18 (Day 7), May 11 reverts to 'single unclaimed event' rather than start of new pattern. **The Houthi non-action persists through the weekend during which Trump-Iran is publicly hardening — Houthi quiet during a US kinetic-preparation phase is structurally significant intelligence, not coincidence.** Container freight FBX index update not available through weekend (weekly cadence). France Charles de Gaulle CSG continues Red Sea presence per prior. **PRIOR (May 13):** Day 81 — non-event POSSIBLY BROKEN May 11. UKMTO reported multiple explosions near a vessel ~130 km SW of Hodeidah; vessel continued voyage, no injuries; Houthis did NOT claim (CONFIRMED ACTION + UNCONFIRMED attribution). First Red Sea incident with possible Houthi attribution since Iran war's 2026 phase began. UKMTO maintains 'moderate' (not critical) for Bab el-Mandeb pending attribution. **NEW TRIGGER WATCH: second Red Sea shipping incident within 7 days of May 11 would collapse the Houthi non-action read.** Until then: single incident, sample size 1. France's Charles de Gaulle CSG remains in Red Sea / Gulf of Aden following CMA CGM San Antonio strike — first European naval-presence shift of cycle holds. Container freight FBX: Asia-USWC ~$2,127/FEU; Med ~$3,850/FEU (routing-penalty intact). Soufan coordination-decay thesis under first material test in 80 days but not yet falsified.

The Red Sea neutral-shipping trigger -- the oil-market wildcard -- stays unfired on Day ~104.

**HOUTHI 'COMPLETE BAN ON ENEMY NAVIGATION' (RHETORIC) AFTER THE JUNE 8-9 STRIKES; JUNE 10 SMALL-BOAT INCIDENT SW OF BALHAF (UNATTRIBUTED, LIKELY PIRACY) — STILL NO CONFIRMED HOUTHI ATTACK ON NEUTRAL SHIPPING.** Following the June 8-9 US/Israeli strikes on Iran, the Houthis signalled renewed Red Sea attacks and declared a 'complete ban on enemy navigation' (gCaptain) — RHETORIC. The only operational event this window: UKMTO reported June 10 a cargo vessel southwest of Balhaf, Yemen approached by a small craft with six armed individuals; the embarked security team exchanged fire and the attackers broke off — no casualties, no damage, attribution UNDER INVESTIGATION (The National). A six-man small-boat boarding reads as plausibly PIRACY, NOT a Houthi missile/drone profile — do NOT attribute to Houthis. UKMTO continues to describe the region as highly volatile with sustained GNSS/AIS/VHF interference. No confirmed Houthi attack on neutral shipping; trigger stays watching, watch elevated.

Trigger stays watching, watch elevated; edge to houthis bumped on activation.

recent_signals.headline: **HOUTHI HAIFA 'MARITIME BLOCKADE' FALSIFIED — LLOYD'S JWC EASTERN-MED/HAIFA 7-DAY TRIGGER EXPIRED UNTRIPPED MAY 26 (CONFIRMED NON-EVENT); RED SEA / SUEZ CORRIDOR STAYS QUIET ON THIS THREAD.** The May 19 'long-distance naval blockade' of Haifa reached its first trigger deadline — Lloyd's JWC adds Eastern Mediterranean / Haifa within 7 days, by May 26 — with NO confirmed JWC listing (Listed Areas remain at JWLA-033, March 3, 2026) and NO confirmed kinetic attack on a Haifa-bound vessel. The Lloyd's-JWC Eastern-Med/Haifa trigger (tracked on the houthis node) therefore EXPIRED UNTRIPPED May 26 — moved `watching` → resolved. Analysts (Maritime Executive) note the Houthis lack the naval reach to enforce a Mediterranean blockade directly and can only pressure shipowners who choose to call at Haifa — the cleanest external falsification of the blockade rhetoric. **Negative-space verification holds**: no kinetic Red Sea incident beyond the May 11 unclaimed Hodeidah event; no confirmed Haifa attack. The separate Houthi-kinetic-on-Haifa-bound-shipping trigger (on the houthis node) stays `watching` ~Day 8 of 14 (by 2026-06-02). The ~48h window's kinetic escalation is on the IRAN axis (US self-defense strikes on IRGC mine-laying boats in Hormuz + a Bandar Abbas SAM site) and the LEBANON front (expanded Israeli ground op) — NEITHER is a Red-Sea action; the corridor stays quiet on this thread. **A CONFIRMED non-event — no edge weight changes.**
sources: Maritime Executive — Houthi 'remote blockade' lacks naval reach for Mediterranean enforcement, can only pressure shipowners calling at Haifa
Lloyd's List / Lloyd's Market Association — JWLA-033 (March 3, 2026) unchanged through May 26, no Eastern Med / Haifa addition
ACLED — independent corroboration of no kinetic Haifa-bound vessel attack

CONFIRMED non-event — no edge weight changes.

recent_signals.verification: Houthi Haifa kinetic attack = CONFIRMED ABSENCE / non-event Day 7 of 14 (multi-source maritime tracking); Lloyd's JWC Eastern Med listing change = CONFIRMED ABSENCE Day 7 of 7 (LMA primary; JWLA-033 still operative); Red Sea negative-space = CONFIRMED ABSENCE (no incident beyond May 11 Hodeidah); Lloyd's-JWC trigger expires May 26 UNTRIPPED, NOT today.

recent_signals.headline: **HOUTHIS ANNOUNCED 'MARITIME BLOCKADE' OF HAIFA — CONFIRMED RHETORIC, NO ACTION; REVERSES MONDAY'S DAY 7 NEGATIVE-SPACE UNTRIPPED FRAMING.** Spokesman Saree said Haifa 'has, from the moment of this statement's release, become one of our targets'; Houthi Communications Center for Red Sea Traffic added a 'long-distance naval blockade' would apply. The announcement is framed as response to Israeli Gaza operations, NOT Iran-US kinetic preparation — preserving Houthi optionality in the Iran-US theater. **Critical structural observation**: Haifa is on the Israeli Mediterranean coast, far from Houthi missile/drone reach from Yemen — the 'long-distance' framing is more rhetorical than operational. Red Sea negative-space window from May 11 Hodeidah closed UNTRIPPED on May 18 Day 7; the Saree announcement re-opens a Mediterranean-axis test rather than a Red Sea-axis re-escalation. No new UKMTO advisory on Red Sea shipping through Tuesday morning IST. **Atlantic Council / Stimson framing**: 'partial regional ceasefires merely redirect rather than resolve hostilities.' Lloyds JWC EastMed AWRP movement will be the cleanest test of whether shipping community is pricing the Saree claim as credible.
recent_signals.verification: Houthi Haifa announcement = CONFIRMED RHETORIC (multi-source primary); operational reach to Mediterranean = REPORTED CLAIM not confirmed by independent maritime tracking; Red Sea negative-space holds — no new UKMTO advisory.

May 11 incident reverts to 'single unclaimed event,' not start of new pattern. Trigger on strait-of-hormuz node moves watching → untripped_expired. **Houthi non-action during publicly-hardening US kinetic-preparation phase is the cleanest behavioural divergence from March-April pattern** (NYT preparation, Trump 'clock is ticking', Trump-Netanyahu call, Saudi drone intercept, UAE Barakah strike all on the same weekend). Pair with TASS/Xinhua non-amplification + Mojtaba Day 11 silence + Hezbollah calibrated attrition = coordinated quietism across Iran's proxy and patron networks against US kinetic noise. **Investor read: Iran signalling escalation control, not escalation.** Container freight FBX01 Asia-US WC ~+2% w/w; Asia-US East Coast +10% same week; Asia-N Europe -3%; Asia-Med +7%. France Charles de Gaulle CSG continues Red Sea presence per prior.

recent_signals.headline: DAY 81 — RED SEA NON-EVENT POSSIBLY BROKEN MAY 11. UKMTO advisory recorded multiple explosions near a vessel ~130 km SW of Hodeidah on Monday May 11; vessel continued voyage, no injuries (CONFIRMED ACTION). **Houthis did NOT claim — attribution UNCONFIRMED.** First Red Sea shipping incident with possible Houthi attribution since the cycle began. New trigger window opens (7 days from May 11) — a second incident would collapse the Houthi non-action read. UKMTO 'moderate' threat level holds pending attribution. France's Charles de Gaulle CSG geometry unchanged.

FRANCE CHARLES DE GAULLE CSG TRANSITED SUEZ TO RED SEA / GULF OF ADEN FOLLOWING CMA CGM SAN ANTONIO STRIKE — FIRST EUROPEAN NAVAL SHIFT OF CYCLE; Macron called for Hormuz reopening AND spoke with Iranian President Pezeshkian (CONFIRMED — French armed forces, Naval News, ABC/NBC, US News, Washington Times). Paris: 'defensive step in full respect of international law' to 'reassure regional partners and support maritime security.' Day 76-77 of Houthi quiet sustained. FBX Med ~$3,850/FEU (+15% week — routing-penalty intact).

historical_summaries.summary: 1 signals. Houthis entered the war with a missile at Israel on March 28 but have NOT attacked Red Sea shipping (yet)

13 signals. Day 33: STILL zero confirmed Houthi attacks on Red Sea commercial shipping | Day 35: Still zero confirmed Houthi attacks on Red Sea commercial shipping | Day 38 of war — zero confirmed Houthi Red Sea shipping attacks
